parsed_review.summary: CP00-664 is a descriptor-only RP21 Admin Console "Test And Golden Case Set" tail slice (M07 S03-S12, 10 units, risk A). Read-only inspection of the contract descriptor, registry bindings/requirements, service builders (case set, descriptor, Hermes/Claude packets, contract projection), validators (coverage + descriptor + projection deep-equality), tests, validator script, and the live closeout-pack-plan confirms full internal consistency: head=CP00-664, range S03-S12, upstream CP00-663, next CP00-665/S13; deliverable distribution {security_audit:2,test:1,ui:1,implementation:6} matches across plan, manifest, requirements, and binding; all 6 required public exports exist. The descriptor-only / no-write / no-real-data / runtime-closed boundaries hold at both the descriptor level (synthetic_only, no_real_data, ~31 enforced false runtime/leak flags, no fixture or real tenant/user/matter/document payloads) and the code level (admin source imports no fs/network/db modules and only builds frozen objects). Claude remains a read-only, non-final reviewer (promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, read_only:true, advisory blocks_pack_on_p0_p1_p2) and Hermes is used only for deterministic review packet/receipt handling (hermes_runtime_opened:false, no runtime receipt) — not product runtime. The pre-review gate state is correct and non-premature: manifest production_ready=false/runtime_ready=false/status=in_progress, inspection verdict PENDING_REVIEW, adjudication pending, readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON (1469 bytes), validation-report within prompt-size/source-inspection/forbidden-tool limits, and the security/audit deliverables correctly force summary-only review off. No P0/P1/P2 issues found. One non-blocking P3 bookkeeping nuance on changed-file-scope classification of the regenerated plan/queue files. Provenance: validation-command exit codes are taken from command-evidence.json (reported; not re-run, since Bash is disallowed in this read-only review), while the contract/code/test consistency and boundary checks are from my direct inspection and are structurally enforced by the validator script's on-disk-contract = computed-projection deep-equality assertion. This review does not constitute final human/enterprise-trust approval, which remains required and outside this receipt; that expected authority boundary does not block this CP closeout.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json classifies regenerated plan/queue files as unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json and docs/closeout-pack-plan/next-pack-queue.json are listed under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved even though they are the pack-lifecycle plan/queue files regenerated by `npm run closeout-pack-plan:generate` (per command-evidence.json) and the manifest plan_ref points at closeout-pack-plan.json. The manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved omits both files entirely, so the two artifacts categorize them differently. This is a cosmetic provenance/labeling nuance only: neither artifact treats these shared files as active_pack_implementation_refs, the operational intent (preserve, do not revert) is correct, and the live_state (CP00-664 head, next S13) is consistent with an open pack pending review. No effect on production_ready support or the no-write/no-real-data boundary.
findings.recommendation: Optionally align the two artifacts by either (a) labeling the regenerated plan/queue as a distinct 'pack-lifecycle generated' category rather than 'unrelated', or (b) listing them in the manifest's preserved set for symmetry. No action required for this closeout.
